sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च शृणुयादपि यो नरः।
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभान् लोकान्प्राप्नुयात् पुण्यकर्मणाम्॥
transliteration: śraddhāvān anasūyaś ca śṛṇuyād api yo naraḥ | so ’pi muktaḥ śubhān lokān prāpnuyāt puṇya-karmaṇām ||
literal_meaning: A faithful, non-envious person who merely hears (the Gītā) is liberated and attains the auspicious worlds of the righteous.
contextual_meaning: Even passive, open-minded listening to sacred truth seeds unconscious healing; faith without resentment dissolves psychic toxins and re-orients the psyche toward pro-social, life-affirming values.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic openness—mind has survived the battle and now seeks integration; ego defenses soften enough to allow benevolent archetypes (Kṛṣṇa) to enter.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out healthcare worker, skeptical of religion, overhears a colleague softly reciting poetry that mirrors the Gītā’s message; instead of mocking, they pause, listen, and feel an unexpected lightness—first hint that cynicism is not permanent.
anchor_text: Listen with trust and without envy; even hearing truth quietly rewires the mind toward freedom.
